stories.headline: Bitcoin-USD correlation hits -0.90, most negative since 2022
stories.tldr: BTC's 30-day correlation with the U.S. Dollar Index has flipped to -0.90, the deepest inverse reading in nearly four years.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Bitcoin is once again trading as a clean dollar-debasement hedge, putting allocators back in macro-narrative mode just as the Fed-cut debate heats up.
the_details: 30-day BTC/DXY correlation hit -0.90, deepest inverse print since 2022.
BTC settled above $77K and is up over 13% in April, on track for its best month in a year.
Push toward $80K stalled as Strait of Hormuz oil risk re-tightened financial conditions.

stories.headline: Solana ETF AUM tops $1B; SOL beats ETH on dApp revenue 5 weeks running
stories.tldr: Spot SOL ETFs crossed $1B in combined AUM with $35M weekly inflows, while Solana outearned Ethereum in dApp revenue for a 5th straight week.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Institutional SOL exposure is sticking even as price lags, and the revenue lead over Ethereum strengthens the case for a multi-L1 institutional bid.
the_details: Combined U.S. spot SOL ETF assets cleared $1B, with $35.17M net inflows last week.
Goldman Sachs disclosed a sizable Solana ETF position in 13F filings.
SOL trades around $85.97, down 32% from 2025 highs; bulls need to reclaim $86 to avoid a $52 retest.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai pushed to 2027 over UAE security risks
stories.tldr: Organizers postponed TOKEN2049 Dubai to 2027, citing heightened security risks across the UAE amid regional tensions.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The Middle East was crypto's hottest 2026 conference circuit; a one-year delay reroutes deal-making capital toward Singapore, Hong Kong and U.S. events.
the_details: TOKEN2049 Dubai shelved until 2027; Singapore flagship still on calendar.
Move follows Strait of Hormuz oil-route flare-ups already weighing on risk assets.
Consensus Miami in May becomes the next major institutional gathering point.

stories.headline: FDIC opens GENIUS Act rulebook for federally supervised stablecoin issuers
stories.tldr: The FDIC issued a notice of proposed rulemaking laying out capital, custody and disclosure requirements for FDIC-supervised payment stablecoin issuers.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This is the first concrete federal rulebook for U.S. dollar stablecoins under GENIUS — a green light for bank-issued stables and a wedge against offshore issuers.
the_details: FDIC NPR sets capital, segregation and reporting bars for permitted payment stablecoin issuers.
Treasury is running a parallel implementation track for non-bank stablecoin licensees.
Move arrives as USDT supply nears $150B and Circle pushes deeper into U.S. distribution.

stories.headline: Coinbase's Pollak: AI agent payments are crypto's next adoption wave
stories.tldr: Coinbase Base lead Jesse Pollak says AI agents transacting on-chain are the next major use case driving crypto payments, ahead of his Consensus Miami keynote.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Stablecoin rails plus autonomous agents could shift micropayment volume on-chain — Coinbase is positioning Base as the default agent settlement layer.
the_details: Pollak signaled the AI-agent thesis ahead of Consensus Miami in May.
Bet leans on Base's USDC integration and Coinbase's MPC wallet infra for agents.
Aligns with broader stablecoin growth — USDT supply is approaching $150B.
